# SBDC Bilingual and Language Support Routing

> BOSS Libraries public routing record. Use it to find the right source, link, organization, or person.

## Description

## Known language-support signals from UH SBDC pages UH SBDC advising pages identify Spanish-speaking advisors and additional advisors with Vietnamese, French, Cantonese, Hindi, and Marathi language capabilities across parts of the network. ## BOSS use

## When to surface

Surface when the user's question concerns mentoring and advising, training and this source is relevant to the next document or human handoff.

## Key information

- ## Known language-support signals from UH SBDC pages
- UH SBDC advising pages identify Spanish-speaking advisors and additional advisors with Vietnamese, French, Cantonese, Hindi, and Marathi language capabilities across parts of the network.
- ## BOSS use
- Ask for language preference when it could improve service access. Route Spanish-language users to Spanish webinars and note the need for bilingual advising in the handoff summary.
- ## Suggested phrasing
- "Would you prefer English, Spanish, or another language for advising or training resources? I can note that in the SBDC handoff."

## People and links

- Organizations: Small Business Development Center (SBDC), University of Houston
- Link: <https://www.sbdc.uh.edu/sbdc/Consulting1.asp>

## Next handoff

Open the cited source link when available and route follow-up to Small Business Development Center (SBDC) or the named qualified resource.

## Guardrails

- The BOSS Libraries Librarian retrieves and routes information; it is not a mentor or a substitute for a qualified professional.
- Verify live pages for current eligibility, deadlines, contacts, prices, and program rules before presenting them as current.

Use **View original** in the BOSS Library to open or download the approved public source.
